typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (plain Python, no mypy)"

blockingIssues.description: Operator normalization can crash on non-string operator values because `_normalize_operators` calls `op.lower()` without a type guard.

Null coercion is incomplete versus stated requirement: several schema-required string fields (for example `source.standard`, `source.section`, `metadata.extracted_by`, `metadata.qc_status`, `data.expression`) are not coerced when null.
Deduplication happens before ID normalization, so `post_process` can normalize distinct IDs into duplicates after dedupe and violate unique-ID expectations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify and enforce consistency for `followedProcedure`: if required verification steps are skipped (such as mandatory real-data checks), workers should set `followedProcedure` to false and record the deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two independent reviews (`schema-definition-type` and `post-processor-module`) found required-skill-step mismatches while handoff still reported `followedProcedure: true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit integration rule: if any transform can mutate IDs, perform final deduplication after that transform (or normalize IDs before dedupe) to preserve unique-ID invariants.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of `post-processor-integration` found duplicate IDs can appear because dedupe currently occurs before ID normalization.

rejectedObservations.observation: Procedure-adherence signaling appears inconsistent: required real-data verification was not run while `followedProcedure` remained true.
